chunk:
CHUNK:015/021
\u201cAre you going to tell me what we\u2019re doing?\u201d I asked as I teased a little more power out of the motors and powered out of Dreamcaster\u2019s shadow. \u201cSure,\u201d Rachael said, although she said nothing more for another five minutes. I coughed a couple of times to nudge her, but Rachael seemed content to look at the scenery as we sailed closer and closer to Qassiarsuk. \u201cYou know, my daddy had a boat,\u201d she said. \u201cBack in Canada.\u201d \u201cYou\u2019re Canadian?\u201d \u201cSure.\u201d Rachael nodded. \u201cFor my sins.\u201d \u201cBut you want to be American?\u201d \u201cYeah, but for Sean \u2013 nothing sinful.\u201d We had yet to be introduced, and my mind wandered as I wondered who the mysterious Sean Augustus Arnet really was, and what hold he had over Rachael to make her kidnap a police officer to complete whatever assignment he had given her. It might have helped if I had met him, but somehow I imagined he would be difficult to fathom, and that without Rachael to help us, we \u2013 the Greenland police and whoever the Americans had sent to take Sean into custody \u2013 never would. \u201cSo, we had a boat,\u201d Rachael said, picking up from where she left off, \u201cWe sailed it on Lake Ontario.\u201d Rachael smiled at the memory and said, \u201cBack then, when I was just a kid, those were my first days of smuggling.\u201d \u201cYour father was a smuggler?\u201d \u201cSure,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cWe would take packets of Smarties from Canada to New York state and sell them to the friends and acquaintances Daddy made round and about. You know Smarties? They\u2019re like M&Ms only they taste different, and they came in these tiny boxes\u2026\u201d Rachael pinched an imaginary box between her finger and thumb. \u201cNot much bigger than a matchbox. The American kids thought they were cute, and they wanted more. So we loaded up each time we went to the store, and then Daddy got me to sell them to the kids on the beaches of New York State.\u201d Rachael drifted for a moment and then smiled. \u201cGood times,\u201d she said. I waited to hear if she had more stories, but Rachael said nothing more before jabbing her finger in the air suddenly and pointing at the site of the Norse ruins of Brattahl\u00ed\u00f0, including the reconstructed longhouse and church. \u201cTake us over there,\u201d she said. \u201cTo the Viking ruins?\u201d \u201cYep,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cThat\u2019s our first stop.\u201d I steered towards Brattahl\u00ed\u00f0, resisting the urge to look over my shoulder to see if Gaba was coming, and then smiled as I wondered what Gaba would say when he realised I was going to get another dose of culture and history. \u201cAnd what do we do when we get there?\u201d Rachael smiled and said, \u201cPatience, Petra. You\u2019ll find out soon enough.\u201d \u201cI guess I will,\u201d I said, choosing my course and following it. \n 12 It was after midnight. The only red and yellow jackets on Qassiarsuk belonged to Rachael and me. We waved at one of the residents walking their dog, and Rachael quickly tucked the MP5 inside her jacket when a small bundle of kids ran past us. I might have used the moment to tackle Rachael, to disarm her and her bring her back to the ship, but not with the kids around. And besides, I was working on the idea that Gaba wanted Rachael to finish her errands as much as Rachael did. And then, of course, the real reason I didn\u2019t take advantage of a moment of surprise was because I was too curious. Rachael wanted something inside the longhouse, and in that moment, no matter how much Paniula\u2019s diary teased me with its secrets, I wanted it too. I felt like something invisible was pulling me along the dusty midnight trails in Qassiarsuk, and if I wanted satisfaction, I just had to let events unfold. Rachael needed me to drive the boat, and Gaba needed me to help Rachael. At least, that was how I chose to interpret what Gaba might want, although his whispered lightweight still had me wondering what I was missing. But then we were at the longhouse, and I followed Rachael inside. The door looked like it was built into the earth itself, and visitors would be forgiven if they thought the triangular roof had been grown, not built by hand. The grass was a lush green, providing a glimpse of the colour the Vikings had used to name the land. Sure, it was a reconstruction, but it was a good one, and I felt a tingle on my skin as we stepped inside. The interior was stone, wood, and reindeer fur, with pine supports that the original Vikings must have brought with them, for even back then, there were no trees in Greenland. The Inuit, my ancestors, had used driftwood for the frames of qajaqs and for other simple structures and tools, but the Vikings were the real artisans when it came to working with wood. \u201cIt\u2019s your first time, isn\u2019t it?\u201d Rachael said as she watched me take it all in. \u201cYes.\u201d And if I hadn\u2019t felt the tingle on my skin, I might have picked up on what Rachael said, that it was my first time, but not hers. As it was, she told me a minute or so later, after I had gotten my fill of Viking heritage. \u201cI was like you the first time,\u201d she said. \u201cHell, I know it\u2019s a reconstruction, but when you look at the village, it\u2019s not a stretch to go back a couple hundred years and imagine how life must have been in the summer, before they all died that winter.\u201d \u201cYou\u2019ve been in Greenland before?\u201d I said, still catching up. \u201cTwice,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cAnd I bet that\u2019s got you all razzled again, eh?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said, aware that my so-called curious frown was in overdrive. \u201cI came here with Sean,\u201d Rachael said.

CTX: CHUNK015/021
SCENE: BOAT NEAR QASSIARSUK; NIGHT
AGENT: PETRA DRIVER
AGENT2: RACHAEL PASSENGER
PETRA: "ARE YOU GOING TO TELL ME WHAT WE’RE DOING?"
RACHAEL: "SURE"
PETRA POWERS MOTORS → EXIT DREAMCASTER-SHADOW
RACHAEL SILENT 5-MIN
PETRA COUGHS TO NUDGE; RACHAEL WATCHES SCENERY
RACHAEL: "YOU KNOW, MY DADDY HAD A BOAT; BACK IN CANADA."
PETRA: "YOU’RE CANADIAN?"
RACHAEL: "SURE ∧ FOR MY SINS."
PETRA: "BUT YOU WANT TO BE AMERICAN?"
RACHAEL: "YEAH, BUT FOR SEAN—NOTHING SINFUL."
PETRA MUSES ON SEAN AUGUSTUS ARNET ∧ HIS HOLD ON RACHAEL
RACHAEL: "SO, WE HAD A BOAT; WE SAILED IT ON LAKE-ONTARIO."
RACHAEL SMILES AT MEMORY
RACHAEL: "BACK THEN, AS A KID, THOSE WERE MY FIRST DAYS OF SMUGGLING."
PETRA: "YOUR FATHER WAS A SMUGGLER?"
RACHAEL: "SURE"
RACHAEL-SMUGGLING-METHOD:
  TAKE SMARTIES FROM CANADA → NY-STATE
  SELL TO ACQUAINTANCES
SMARTIES≈MATCHBOX-SIZE PACKAGES
AMERICAN-KIDS DEMAND↑ SMARTIES
SELL ON NY-STATE BEACHES
RACHAEL: "GOOD TIMES."
RACHAEL POINTS AT BRATTAHLÍÐ NORSE-RUINS
PETRA: "TO THE VIKING RUINS?"
RACHAEL: "YEP; FIRST STOP."
PETRA STEERS TOWARD BRATTAHLÍÐ ∧ WONDER GABA’S RESPONSE
PETRA: "WHAT DO WE DO WHEN WE GET THERE?"
RACHAEL: "PATIENCE, PETRA; YOU’LL FIND OUT SOON ENOUGH."
PETRA FOLLOWS COURSE
FUTURE: POST-MIDNIGHT ARRIVAL QASSIARSUK
ATTIRE: JACKETS=RED/YELLOW WORN BY PETRA∧RACHAEL
WAVE AT RESIDENT WALKING DOG
RACHAEL TUCKS MP5 INSIDE JACKET AS KIDS RUN PAST
PETRA CONSIDERS DISARMING RACHAEL ∧ ABORTS DUE TO KIDS∧CURIOSITY
PETRA ASSUMES GABA WANTS RACHAEL FINISH ERRANDS
PETRA CURIOUS ABOUT LONGHOUSE CONTENTS ∧ PANIULA’S DIARY
INVISIBLE-FORCE PULLS PETRA ALONG MIDNIGHT-TRAILS
PETRA YIELDS TO UNFOLDING EVENTS
PETRA DRIVES BOAT; SUPPORTS RACHAEL FOR GABA
PETRA FOLLOWS RACHAEL INTO LONGHOUSE
LONGHOUSE DOOR IN EARTH; ROOF COVERED IN GRASS
INTERIOR: STONE∧WOOD∧REINDEER-FUR; PINE SUPPORTS IMPORTED BY VIKINGS
INUIT-ANCESTORS USED DRIFTWOOD FOR QAJAQ FRAMES
VIKINGS=WOODWORK ARTISANS
RACHAEL: "IT’S YOUR FIRST TIME, ISN’T IT?"
PETRA: "YES."
RACHAEL: "I WAS LIKE YOU FIRST TIME."
PETRA FEELS VIKING-HERITAGE TINGLE
RACHAEL: "IMAGINE SUMMER-VILLAGE BEFORE WINTER-DEATHS."
PETRA: "YOU’VE BEEN IN GREENLAND BEFORE?"
RACHAEL: "TWICE."
RACHAEL: "I BET THAT’S GOT YOU RAZZLED, EH?"
PETRA: "YES."
RACHAEL: "I CAME HERE WITH SEAN."
